name: MemEngine
tagline: A unified, modular library for building advanced memory in LLM-based agents
seoTitle: MemEngine - Modular Memory Library for LLM Agents
metaDescription: MemEngine is a Python library that implements many research agent-memory models under one interface, so you can build, switch, and compare memory designs.
overview: MemEngine is a Python library for developing the memory layer of LLM-based agents. It brings together memory models from recent research papers and implements them under one unified, modular framework, so you can use a single interface instead of reimplementing each design from scratch.

It is aimed at researchers and engineers who want to add memory to agents and compare different approaches. Memory modules organize their logic into hierarchical levels where higher-level modules reuse lower-level ones, and a configuration module lets you adjust hyper-parameters and prompts without rewriting code.

Within the agent-memory space, MemEngine focuses on being pluggable. Its memory modules expose common operations like store and recall, can be dropped into existing agent frameworks, and support local use as a Python package or remote use as a service behind a port.
features: Unified, modular framework with three hierarchical levels where higher-level modules reuse lower-level ones
Many published agent-memory models implemented under one interface, so you can switch and test them with different configs
Configuration module for adjusting hyper-parameters and prompts at different levels
Pluggable memory modules with simple store and recall operations that work across agent frameworks
Default, configurable, and automatic memory usage modes
Local deployment as a Python library or remote deployment as a service via uvicorn and FastAPI

gettingStarted.intro: Install MemEngine into a Python 3.9+ environment, then create a memory module and use its store and recall operations inside your agent.

steps.heading: Install from source (recommended)
steps.body: The maintainers recommend installing from source to get the latest version. Create a Python 3.9 environment, clone the repo, and install in editable mode.
steps.code:
conda create -n memengine_env python=3.9
git clone https://github.com/nuster1128/MemEngine.git
cd MemEngine
pip install -e .

steps.heading: Or install from pip
steps.body: If you prefer a packaged release, install from PyPI. Note this may not be the latest version.
steps.code:
conda create -n memengine_env python=3.9
pip install memengine

steps.heading: Create a memory module and use it
steps.body: Import a memory model such as FUMemory with its config, then call recall to fetch context and store to save new observations. This mirrors the dialogue-agent example from the README.
steps.code:
from memengine.config.Config import MemoryConfig
from memengine.memory.FUMemory import FUMemory

memory = FUMemory(MemoryConfig(DialogueAgentMemoryConfig))

# fetch relevant context for the current input
context = memory.recall(observation)

# save a new turn into memory
memory.store('%s: %s' % (role, response))

useCases: Adding persistent memory to a conversational agent so it can recall earlier turns
Comparing several published memory models on the same task by switching configs
Prototyping a new memory model by extending the library's operations and functions
Running memory as a shared service behind a port for multiple agents to use
